reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Confirmed the missing validation. The by_range handler at rpc_methods.rs:1148 checks `req.max_requested() > max_request_data_column_sidecars`, but the by_root handler at rpc_methods.rs:408-460 omits this check. Decode-time validation at codec.rs:567 only limits the number of identifiers to max_request_blocks_deneb (128), not the total column count across all identifiers. Each DataColumnsByRootIdentifier can contain up to NumberOfColumns (128) column indices. An attacker can send 128 identifiers × 128 columns = 16384 total columns, exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS (16384). Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-pre-009 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:588-604 requires 'data_column_sidecars_by_root must reject requests exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS' with assertion 'forall req: sum(len(r.columns) for r in req) > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS => rejected(req)'. The code does NOT reject such requests, violating the spec. Severity: Medium is appropriate. Single-packet P2P attack causing resource exhaustion (DB lookups, KZG ops, serialization) on targeted node, but unlikely to bring down >5% of network. Not a 'high-effort DoS' (requires only 1 packet). An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending a single DataColumnsByRoot request with 128 block roots each requesting max columns, causing excessive resource consumption for 16384 column retrievals/responses.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_sidecars_by_root must reject requests exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS. (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:588)
